what: M=64 fused path: waves_per_eu=4 (from 2). v129 tested waves=4 with .cg cache modifier (+0.8% neutral). v221 tested waves=3 with cache=None (+0.9%). waves=4 with cache=None never tested. AMD library non-preshuffle config uses waves=4 for M<=64 N=7168 K=2048. Higher occupancy hint may better utilize CUs when cache=None allows L1 hits, reducing register-file pressure impact.
why: M=64 is the biggest shape (13.2 us, ~28% of geomean). waves_per_eu=4 combined with cache=None has never been tested. The cache modifier change fundamentally alters how data flows through the cache hierarchy, which changes the optimal occupancy hint.

what_failed: waves_per_eu=4 for M=64 fused path with cache=None (+1.9% regression). Extensive analysis of 233+ versions shows exhausted parameter space: all BSM/BSN/BSK/NW/NS/waves_per_eu/cache_modifier/GROUP_SIZE_M/matrix_instr_nonkdim values tested for all 3 execution paths. XCD remap hurts small-block shapes. Non-preshuffle kernels have bad memory access patterns. Gluon kernel has server compatibility issues. ASM tile sizes confirmed optimal by tuned CSV. Split-K reduce explored with 5+ block size configs. Dispatch overhead optimizations (closures, precompute, torch.compile) all neutral. Custom HIP/ISA kernels hit server compilation timeouts.
